title: Algebraic K-theory of stable infinity-categories satisfies excision
abstract: We prove that algebraic K-theory, viewed as a functor on stable ∞-categories, satisfies a strong excision property along arbitrary localising sequences.
ai_proof_review:
## Setup

The claim sharpens the additivity theorem of Waldhausen and the localisation theorem of Thomason–Trobaugh, extending excision to a much broader setting.

## Lemma 3.7 (filtered colimit lemma)

The proof uses a clever fibre-sequence comparison. The construction is intricate but each diagram I checked commutes. I am, however, not fully convinced of the coherence data in the cube of $\infty$-categories on p. 17 — it is stated as 'evident' but the higher coherences are non-trivial.

## Theorem A

Follows from Lemma 3.7 and the standard machinery. The gap, if there is one, is in 3.7's coherence data.

## Overall

The result, if correct, is a major structural theorem people have wanted. The proof is plausible but the coherence handwave on p. 17 is the kind of place where errors hide in higher-categorical work. Verifying that step requires a careful $(\infty, 2)$-categorical bookkeeping that the paper does not do explicitly.
